summary: Started implementation with a runnable MVP. Baseline calibration, Bayesian fusion, linguistic text cues, and WAV-based audio cues are now working. Video, visual, physiological, and adapter layers remain unbuilt.

completed.details: Added Python package entrypoints and __init__ files for core, modalities, and blitz_engine.
Implemented PersonalBaseline recording, per-cue robust statistics, shrinkage, and quality reporting in core/calibration/baseline.py.
Implemented cue weighting, posterior fusion, uncertainty estimate, channel contributions, and top-cue ranking in core/fusion/bayesian_fusion.py.

Added modalities/linguistic/analyzer.py with heuristic text cues: filler ratio, pronoun avoidance, distancing language, qualifier overload, negative emotion density, sensory detail poverty, lexical diversity drop, and response delay.
Added blitz_engine/engine.py exposing BlitzEngine and BlitzSession with baseline creation and analyze_text flow.
Added pyproject.toml for editable local installation.
Added blitz CLI support for analyze-text plus stable JSON serialization helpers.
Added modalities/audio/analyzer.py and multimodal session support for WAV-based audio cues.

current_limitations: Only text and WAV audio modalities are implemented.
The global convergence gate still requires 2 modality families, so text-only runs can score high but will not pass the gate.
No FastAPI app or dataset harness exists yet.
Cue extraction is heuristic and dependency-light; it is not benchmarked.

next_recommended_steps.reason: A local API is the next adapter needed for browser or UI work.

next_recommended_steps.task: Expand tests beyond the current text MVP smoke coverage
next_recommended_steps.reason: There is now a basic regression test, but fusion and calibration need more direct assertions.
Visual analysis is still absent and will matter for multi-family convergence.

next_recommended_steps.task: Refine audio feature extraction and add transcription support
next_recommended_steps.reason: The current WAV cues are simple proxies; the next lift is richer speech-derived features.
